what_it_does: This file is a generated address registry that enumerates files and directories under the project root, recording each address, kind, parent container, edge relationship, extension, special name, byte size, and attachments, along with registry-level rules and metadata.
observations: The registry contains 669 entries rooted at /home/tim/repos/counterpart/design.
Each entry uses its relative path as the key and declares kind (directory or file), parent, and edge_from_parent.
Directories carry type (root, dir, dir.dotfile) while files carry name, extension, special_name, bytes, and attachments.
The prime axis is contains (directory -> file|directory) and every typed edge is supposed to declare a directional inverse.
ignore_patterns exclude source/**, reference/**, .git/**, node_modules/**, review/**(catalog-only), and registry/**(generated).
Valid registries are listed: addresses, folder-types, extension-types, special-names, languages, projects, attachment-types.

Visible data includes root files (.gitignore, PROJECT.md, FORMATION_JOURNAL.md, ONBOARDING.md, CLAUDE.md), the app/ HTML files, factory/output/ JSON artifacts, and a large review/ image collection with nested ingest/ folders.
Attachments arrays are present but empty in every visible file entry.
standouts: factory/output/ holds many structured JSON outputs grouped into subdirectories (dials, templates, components).
review/ingest/capital-raise/ contains a long sequence of numbered page images, suggesting imported reference material.
CLAUDE.md and .gitignore are flagged with special_name values, while most files have null.
The registry embeds its own schema rules (prime_axis, equal_opposite, valid_registries) inside the same ledger.
conventions: Address keys match relative paths from the project root.
Parent references form a containment tree via the contains edge.
File entries consistently include bytes and an attachments array, even when empty.
Special names are explicitly tagged for known dotfiles and project meta files.
Directory entries omit bytes and attachments, distinguishing them from file entries.
concerns: The provided file is truncated, so full coverage and consistency cannot be verified.
ignore_patterns marks registry/** as generated, yet this file lives in registry/, making the exclusion self-referential.
The equal_opposite rule is stated but no inverse edges are visible in the data shown.
Attachments are empty in all visible entries, so their intended use is unclear from this file alone.
_meta lacks a generation timestamp or checksum.
notes: A generated directory ledger that maps every project path to a typed containment node with metadata, serving as the canonical address index for downstream design-system registries.
questions: What process generates this registry and how often is it refreshed?
What kinds of data are intended to populate the attachments arrays?
How and where are the directional inverse edges for equal_opposite recorded?
Do the ignored source/** and reference/** directories still appear in other registries or are they completely invisible?
How do the seven valid_registries relate to one another and to this address registry?
purpose_vs_actual: It appears to be a generated address index; the visible data matches that purpose, though the file is truncated so full coverage cannot be confirmed.
apparent_themes: separation of source, reference, and generated artifacts
intention_signals: The file tries to establish a stable, queryable address space for the design system by giving every path a typed identity, parent link, and attachment slot, enabling graph traversal and cross-registry correlation.
novelty: The ledger explicitly encodes registry rules (ignore patterns, prime axis, equal-opposite edges, valid registries) inside the same file as the address data, making the schema self-describing.
contribution: It contributes a complete address map and self-documented schema conventions that a fused system could use to resolve paths, infer containment, and anchor other registries without relying on filesystem enumeration.
summary_for_embedding: A generated JSON address registry listing 669 files and directories under counterpart/design with kind, parent, edge, extension, size, and attachment metadata plus registry rules.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a canonical, machine-readable containment graph of the design repository for cross-referencing and registry generation.
